frozen_components: MLLM backbone (inference-time weights fixed during RL)
architecture.summary: SVoT is a reinforcement learning framework that augments multimodal large language models (MLLMs) with interleaved text and visual reasoning over spatial state sequences. The model generates intermediate states (text descriptions and visualizations) alongside transition reasoning chains, enabling verification of action preconditions and effects. A trained reward function evaluates the correctness of generated states and transitions. The MLLM backbone is fine-tuned via Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to maximize rewards that capture both state accuracy and transition validity.
trained_components: reward function for state and transition verification

biblio.title: SVoT: State-aware Visualization-of-Thought for Spatial Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning

claims.dataset: out-of-distribution test sets across five spatial reasoning domains
claims.statement: SVoT with transition-aware supervision achieves state-of-the-art performance across the introduced domains, yielding up to a 65% absolute accuracy gain on out-of-distribution test sets.
SVoT integrates transition reasoning chains into the generation processes, enabling the model to verify action preconditions and effects through interleaved textual and visual reasoning.
SVoT generates interleaved, verifiable intermediate states and visualizations to enable reliable multi-hop inference over both intermediate states and state transitions.
Current studies often leave intermediate states unverified and treat state transitions as implicit processes, which limits reliability in multi-hop spatial reasoning.
Existing benchmarks reduce state transitions to single-variable updates, substantially simplifying the problems.
Five domains are established by extending classical environments and introducing two novel domains, Pacman and Gather, that require multi-object interactions and numerical reasoning.
